exemplar.title: Tversky y Kahneman (1986), El uso del marco positivo y negativo y su efecto en la toma de decisiones (enfoque cognitivo)

detailedExemplarData.title: Tversky y Kahneman (1986), El uso del marco positivo y negativo y su efecto en la toma de decisiones (enfoque cognitivo)

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Aim clearly stated and relevance to real-world adolescent decision-making well explained
Dual-process theory and anchoring heuristic accurately described and explicitly linked to the investigation
Independent and dependent variables are correctly operationalized in the hypothesis
weaknesses: Some phrasing in the relevance explanation is general rather than fully nuanced
Alternative hypothesis contains a wording slip and contradicts earlier rationale
Minor date imprecision in theory description
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The introduction demonstrates strong alignment with the top band on two strands (aim/relevance and theory/model) with clear explanations and connections. The hypothesis/variables strand meets the 3â4 band: operationalization is present but clarity is weakened by a wording slip and contradiction. Overall, 5/6 reflects two solid 5-band performances and one high 4-band performance.

strengths: Research design (independent-measures) is identified and a rationale (avoiding demand characteristics) is given
Sampling technique and participant characteristics are described with basic justification
Controlled variables and materials are listed and their role in internal validity briefly explained
weaknesses: Explanation of design lacks discussion of alternatives and trade-offs
Sampling justification does not address potential bias from opportunity sampling
Some controls (e.g., time pressure) are omitted from the materials/variables section
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration section consistently fits the 3â4 band: research design, sampling, and controls are all explained at a basic level but lack deeper analytical detail. A mid-band score of 3/4 accurately reflects solid description with room for more extensive justification.

strengths: Appropriate descriptive and inferential statistics (means, SDs, MannâWhitney U) applied with correct p-value interpretation
Findings are interpreted with respect to the data and linked back to the hypothesis and dual-process theory
Statistical reasoning (choice of non-parametric test) is sound
weaknesses: Graphical presentation exaggerates differences (scale issue) and lacks error bars
No effect-size calculation; use of means without robust measures given skew
Missing median or other robust statistics for skewed data
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis aligns with the top of the 3â4 band: statistics are appropriate and accurate, and findings are well interpreted, but presentational errors and missing effect-size reporting prevent a 5-6 band score. Therefore, 4/6 is awarded.

strengths: Findings are discussed in reference to background theory and original study
Relevant strengths and limitations are identified and explained with respect to internal and ecological validity
Modifications are clearly linked to stated limitations
weaknesses: Discussion is relatively brief and lacks engagement with alternative explanations or wider literature
Strengths and limitations lack a clear hierarchy of importance
Proposed modifications require more practical detail (e.g., sample sizes, logistics)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation fits the 3â4 band: discussion makes appropriate references to theory, and strengths/limitations and modifications are relevant and linked. However, depth, prioritization, and practical details are limited, justifying a mid-band score of 3/6.

strandSelections.reasoning: A bar graph is provided and it addresses the hypothesis by contrasting group means.  Axes are labelled, but the y-axis scale exaggerates differences and no error bars or median values are shown.  These presentational limitations move it from âwholly correctâ (5-6) to âaddresses hypothesis but contains errorsâ, warranting 3.

Findings are clearly stated (means, p-values) and **linked back to both the hypothesis and the dual-process theory**, demonstrating interpretation rather than mere description.  The explanation is sound but not richly detailed (no discussion of effect size or practical significance), so 4 fits the top of the 3-4 band.

Both IV (order of presentation of the multiplication) and DV (numerical estimate) are **identified and operationalised**; however the alternative hypothesis contains a wording slip (âmayoresâ¦ condiciÃ³n descendente con respecto aâ¦ condiciÃ³n descendenteâ) and the predicted direction contradicts the explanation given earlier.  Because the operationalisation is present but clarity is weakened by the inconsistency, the strand fits the 3-4 band, at the top of that range.

Opportunity sampling is named and participant characteristics (age, language, academic tracks, gender balance) are given.  A short justification (âfÃ¡cil, rÃ¡pido y eficienteâ; mathematics competence) shows some reasoning, but it is not deeply analytical (e.g. no consideration of bias).  This meets the âexplainedâ descriptor at a basic level, so 3/4.

The dual-process model (System 1 vs System 2) and the anchoring heuristic are **accurately described** with appropriate detail (automatic, intuitive processing, origin of biases, etc.).  The student then **explicitly connects** the model to the planned replication of Tversky & Kahneman by arguing that anchoring demonstrates System 1âs influence.  Minor imprecision (dates cited as 1986 instead of 1974 once) stops it being exemplary; therefore 5/6 is awarded.

The student discusses how the significant difference supports System 1 processing and anchoring, referencing the original study.  The discussion, however, is relatively brief and does not engage with alternative explanations or wider literature, matching the descriptor âdescribed with referenceâ rather than a fuller critical discourse.  Hence 4/6.

The student clearly states an aim â to examine whether the order of the factors in a multiplication task (ascending vs descending) influences adolescentsâ numerical estimates â and then **explains why this is relevant**, linking it to everyday decision-making, advertising and adolescentsâ cognitive development.  The justification is explicit rather than merely mentioned, so the work fits the 5-6 band. A few phrases are general (e.g. âpapel crucial en su vida cotidianaâ), preventing a flawless, fully nuanced explanation; hence the lower mark within the top band (5 rather than 6).

Several strengths (high internal validity, standardised instructions) and limitations (low ecological validity, individual maths ability) are **identified and explained**, showing relevance to the study.  Yet some points are superficial or speculative, and there is no hierarchy of importance, so a mid-band score of 3 is suitable.

Controlled variables (time, room, instructions) and materials (projector, slides, consent forms) are identified and the student briefly explains how each control supports internal validity.  The explanations are concise but sufficient for the 3-4 band; some controls (e.g. time pressure) are omitted, so the lower mark in the band is chosen.

Two modifications are offered and each is explicitly linked to a stated limitation, with brief justification (naturalistic purchasing task to raise ecological validity; matched-pairs design to control maths skill).  The links are clear but reasoning is concise and lacks practical detail (sample size, logistics), so 3/4 within this band.

The student identifies an **independent-measures design** and offers a brief rationale (avoiding demand characteristics).  While this constitutes an explanation, it is surface-level and does not consider alternative designs or more detailed methodological implications, so a mid-band (3) score within 3-4 is appropriate.

Appropriate descriptive statistics (means, SDs) and an inferential test (Mann-Whitney U) are used with a reasonable justification for non-parametric choice and independent samples.  Calculations appear accurate and p-values correctly interpreted.  Minor concerns â using means despite clear skew, no effect-size calculation â keep it below the top band but place it at the top of 3-4.

annotations.comment: Including a brief implication or application in the conclusion would strengthen the takeaway beyond restating findings.
The title effectively frames the psychological investigation topic, guiding the reader immediately to the study focus.
Administrative details such as student codes and word count add no academic feedback and should be omitted from annotated commentary.
The description of dual-process theory (System 1 vs System 2) is accurate, detailed, and well connected to heuristics and biases.
The explanation of relevance begins but is cut off; ensure the link between anchoring and adolescentsâ cognitive development is fully articulated.
The rationale for studying order effects in multiplication estimations is clear, but the relevance paragraph could be extended to consider real-world decision contexts.
The independent and dependent variables are operationalized, but clarify that the âanchorâ is the first factor shown, not the entire multiplication.
The alternative hypothesis contains a wording slip and contradicts the earlier rationale; review and correct the direction and wording of H1.
The choice of an independent-measures design is explained, but consider discussing alternative designs (e.g., repeated-measures) and their trade-offs.
Sampling characteristics are well described, but the justification does not address potential bias from opportunity sampling.
The control of environmental variables and standardized instructions supports internal validity; consider also controlling time pressure explicitly.
Descriptive statistics table is clear, but given skewed estimates, including medians or robust measures would give a fuller picture.
The control paragraph is strong in detailing how room, time, and instructions were standardized, which enhances internal validity.
Using mean despite clear outliers warrants justification; consider reporting medians or trimming extreme values for robust analysis.
The bar graph addresses the hypothesis, but the y-axis scale exaggerates effects and lacks error bars; adjust for clarity and accuracy.
The choice of MannâWhitney U is appropriate for non-normal independent data, demonstrating sound statistical reasoning.
An effect-size measure is missing; include an appropriate index (e.g., r for Mann-Whitney) to complement p-values.
P-value interpretation correctly links statistical significance to theory, reinforcing the anchoring effect under System 1.
Findings are interpreted with regard to data and linked to hypothesis and dual-process theory, satisfying strong interpretation criteria.
Discussion references the dual-process model, but should engage alternative explanations or wider literature for deeper critical evaluation.
Strengths and limitations are relevant but lack a hierarchy of importance; consider prioritizing limitations tied to the hypothesis.
Proposed modifications link to limitations, but require more practical detail (e.g., sample sizes, logistics) to be fully justified.
The suggestion to increase ecological validity via a purchase context is appropriate; specify how stimuli would be standardized.
Future modifications mention matching on math ability; consider specifying diagnostic measures and pairing criteria.
References section lacks consistent formatting and some citations are incomplete; ensure alignment with APA or IB style guidelines.
